Operation 'optimizeImagesByUrl' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description lacks critical details about compression modes, authentication requirements, asynchronous processing behavior, and what metadata is returned. Added specifics about supported compression modes, API key requirement, and clarification that processing is asynchronous to help AI agents understand the operation's behavior and when to use it.

data.current_description: Optimizes images accessible via URL. Accepts up to 100 image URLs per request. Returns optimization result URLs and metadata.
data.description_suggestion: Optimizes images accessible via URL with asynchronous processing. Accepts up to 100 image URLs per request. Requires ShortPixel API key for authentication. Returns optimization metadata including result URLs, compression statistics, and processing status. Supports lossy, lossless, and glossy compression modes. Images are processed asynchronously; use returned URLs to access optimized results.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks critical details about compression modes, authentication requirements, asynchronous processing behavior, and what metadata is returned. Added specifics about supported compression modes, API key requirement, and clarification that processing is asynchronous to help AI agents understand the operation's behavior and when to use it.

message: Operation 'optimizeImageSync' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description lacks authentication requirements, supported compression modes, and guidance on when to use this synchronous variant versus the asynchronous batch operation. Added details about API key requirement, compression mode support, and use case differentiation to improve AI agent decision-making about operation selection.

data.current_description: Returns optimized image directly with image content-type header instead of metadata. Only accepts a single image URL.
data.description_suggestion: Optimizes a single image synchronously and returns the optimized image binary directly. Accepts a single image URL and requires ShortPixel API key for authentication. Returns the compressed image with appropriate image content-type header (e.g., image/jpeg, image/png). Supports lossy, lossless, and glossy compression modes. Use this operation when you need immediate image delivery rather than metadata or batch processing.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks authentication requirements, supported compression modes, and guidance on when to use this synchronous variant versus the asynchronous batch operation. Added details about API key requirement, compression mode support, and use case differentiation to improve AI agent decision-making about operation selection.

message: Operation 'optimizeImageByUpload' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description is incomplete regarding authentication, compression modes, and response format. Added clarity about API key requirement, supported compression modes, and that response format varies based on parameters. This helps AI agents understand when to use file upload versus URL-based optimization and what to expect in responses.

data.current_description: Optimizes images via file upload for non-publicly-accessible files. Uses multipart form data.
data.description_suggestion: Optimizes images via file upload for non-publicly-accessible files. Accepts multipart form data with image files and requires ShortPixel API key for authentication. Supports lossy, lossless, and glossy compression modes. Returns optimization metadata including compression statistics and result URLs or binary image data depending on request parameters. Use this operation when images cannot be accessed via public URL.
data.reasoning: Current description is incomplete regarding authentication, compression modes, and response format.
